Draft of Democrats’ Report on 2024 Loss Reopens Wounds Ahead of Midterms
The D.N.C.’s 192-page draft report offers no overarching conclusions, featuring instead a grab-bag of political clichés, well-known truisms about the 2024 defeat and political word salad.

The draft report blames both the Biden and Harris campaigns for failing to successfully drive a negative view of Trump, writing that Democrats had chosen not to “engage in negative advertising at the scale required.”
Notably absent from the 192-page document: any mention of Biden’s age or how voters reacted to Democrats running an octogenarian for president.
The draft report also includes no coverage of the selection of Tim Walz as the vice presidential running mate. The pick has since been second-guessed by some Democrats.
The draft report makes no mention of Israel or Gaza, an issue that dogged Biden and Harris in 2024 and has since fractured the party.

Many of the statements are annotated with red text that notes the lack of evidence for them. A page with the section title “Executive Summary” was blank with a note that reads: “This section was not provided by author.”
In several places, the draft report urges Democrats to eschew “identity politics” in favor of economic issues and efforts to address cost-of-living concerns.
The release of the draft report was an extraordinary turn of events for the party, which all but trashed the document as incomplete and inaccurate, and released it only after months of headaches.
